[JS Weird World] No.2 Quick Concept Check: Name/Value Pairs and Objects
JsWeirdWorld
JavaScript Deep Dive: Conquering the Weird Parts

A name only ever pairs with one single value — same idea as a variable only holding one value at a time. A name can be defined and reassigned many times, but at any given moment it maps to just one value. This post also covers how objects in JavaScript are really just collections of name/value pairs.

The Weird Parts of JS (No.1): Syntax Parsers, Execution Contexts, and Lexical Environments
JavaScript Deep Dive: The Weird Parts of JS

Kicking off a whole series on the weird parts of JavaScript — the language people misunderstand more than any other. In this first post I take notes on three foundational ideas: the syntax parser, the lexical environment, and the execution context. They sound scary, but they're not. Let's untangle them.

What's a Closure in JavaScript? A Beginner-Friendly Walkthrough

People throw around the word "closure" all the time in JavaScript, but what does it actually mean? In short, it's a function declared inside another function. This post walks through closures with a simple example, shows how variable scope pollution causes bugs, and explains how a closure fixes it.

How props and emit Pass Data Between Vue Components
I've been picking up Vue again to build something small, and since I'm splitting things into components, I need to understand how `props` and `emit` pass data between them.

What Does the @ Symbol Mean in Vue? The src Path Alias vs Relative Paths

The @ you keep seeing in Vue import paths is a path alias for the src directory, set up by Vue CLI. This post explains how @ differs from relative paths like .., and where the alias is configured in Vue CLI 2 and 3.

Using WOWJS in a Vue CLI Project
I've been building a small project lately with Vue CLI, so here's a quick note on how to use WOWJS inside a Vue CLI app.
/en/javascript/2019/05/07/javascript-global-vs-local-variables/
Global vs Local Variables in JavaScript: The Trap That Catches Every Beginner

JavaScript is full of little traps that trip up beginners — one wrong move and you've stepped on a landmine. This post walks through the difference between global and local variables, why variable pollution happens, and a few tricky challenge questions involving hoisting and scope so you can really lock down the concept.
/en/javascript/2019/05/05/javascript-pagination-with-fetch/
Building Pagination From Scratch in JavaScript (with the Fetch API)

When a site has a lot of data, you need pagination. Sometimes the backend handles it and just hands you a page at a time — easy. But what if the backend dumps everything to the frontend at once? Then you've got to build the pagination logic yourself. This post walks through doing exactly that: pulling data with the Fetch API and writing the pagination logic by hand.
/en/javascript/2019/04/26/javascript-array-sort-how-it-works-01/
Array.prototype.sort() Isn't As Simple As It Looks

After I wrote a post about 20 ways to work with JavaScript arrays, a friend asked me how sort() actually works under the hood — and honestly, I paused too. So I went digging. This post walks through how sort() compares values, what those a and b arguments really are, why the comparator return value matters, and what happens when you return NaN or undefined.
